prompt: The TreeSet silently drops elements. What is the bug?
choices: TreeSet does not accept custom Comparators
The Comparator compares only by x; Point(1,10) and Point(1,20) have the same x so the Comparator returns 0, meaning they are considered equal; TreeSet discards the duplicate — size is 1
TreeSet requires Comparable elements even when a Comparator is provided
Adding elements with equal keys throws IllegalArgumentException
explanation: TreeSet uses its Comparator (or natural ordering) to determine equality. If comparator.compare(a, b) == 0, the set treats the elements as equal and discards the second. Here x=1 for both, so (1,20) is silently dropped. Fix: compare by both fields: Comparator.comparingInt((Point p)->p.x).thenComparingInt(p->p.y).
